chapter.title: Managing Bus Trajectories
chapter.para: We will, for our examples, use data from STAR which is the Rennes' public transport company. This data is protected by ODbL (Open Database License)\cite{SourceSTAR}.
chapter.section:
Introduction to MobilityDB
Importing the data
You can find in the repository for this workshop a CSV file called Freq_Mars2024 data.csv. It should look like the Figure 1.
First few lines of the table
This is the data we will use for this workshop. If you want more recent data, you can find it here by selecting the month you want.
The columns in the table are listed below, with in the first column af the table the original name (in french), in the second column the english translation and in the third column an explanation of the column:
AIS columns
DateFreq Date Date of the entry, format: 31/12/2024
TrancheHoraire15mn 15 min time slot Beginning of the 15 minutes time slot that encompasses the time of that stop, format: 23:59:59
Timeo Timeo id of the stop for that entry
NomArret Stop Name Name of that stop
NomCommune Town Name Name of the town that stop is in
identifiantLigne Line id id of the line for that entry
NomCourtLigne Line name Human readable name of that line
Sens Direction Direction the bus is going in, 'indéterminé' if it’s not a bus
Frequentation Frequentation An estimation of the real number of people that got on the bus at that stop, based on the number of recorded people and indicators like the fraud rate.
First, we are going to link the MobilityDB extension to the database. To do so, you need to execute the following command in your SQL editor:
CREATE EXTENSION mobilitydb CASCADE; We also need to set the date style to the European style, as the data is in this format. To do so, you need to execute the following command:
SET datestyle TO DMY;
Then, we need to create a table in the database to store this data. Here is how to create the first three columns of the table:
CREATE TABLE RennesInput( DateFreq date, TimeSlot15mn time, Timeo integer, We let you create the rest of the table. See you next page to see the full table!
Here is how to create the full table:
CREATE TABLE RennesInput( DateFreq date, TimeSlot15mn time, Timeo integer, StopName varchar(50), TownName varchar(50), LineID integer, LineName varchar(50), Direction varchar(20), NbPersBoarding varchar(50) );
TODO
